title: cuphea racemosa var. ramosior
intro: Cuphea racemosa var. ramosior, commonly known as the firecracker plant, is a flowering plant species within the Lythraceae family. It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its use in wound healing and as an antiseptic. The bioactive constituents of Cuphea racemosa var. ramosior include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

side_effects: Cuphea racemosa var. ramosior can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues due to its volatile oils and alkaloids. Ingesting the plant's parts can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with medications and worsen underlying health conditions.
